oligos.name: pMENT common oligo
oligos.role: Phosphorylated mosaic-end complement oligo; anneals to the transferred strand of both T5 and T7 tagmentation oligos to form the double-stranded MEDS transposon ends
oligos.notes: 5'-phosphorylated. Reverse complement of the 19-bp mosaic end (ME) AGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG.

oligos.name: T5 tagment sciATAC (MEDS A transferred strand)
oligos.role: Non-barcoded P5-side tagmentation oligo loaded into TnY (MEDS A); its 3' mosaic end is transferred to the P5 end of genomic fragments

components.name: P5-side PCR handle (Nextera Read 1 handle)
components.role: priming site for P5i ATAC-seq PCR1 primer

components.name: linker
components.role: spacer / Read 1 ATACseq primer binding region

components.name: Mosaic End (ME)
components.role: Tn5/TnY transferred mosaic end

oligos.name: ATAC-seq barcoded tagmentation oligo (MEDS B / T7 tagment sciATAC)
oligos.role: Barcoded P7-side tagmentation oligo loaded into TnY (MEDS B); carries the first-round cell barcode (BC1) and is transferred to the P7 end of genomic fragments. 96 barcoded versions.

components.name: P7-side PCR handle
components.role: priming site for P7i ATAC-seq PCR1 primer

components.name: BC1 tagmentation cell barcode
components.role: first-round combinatorial cell barcode (matched to RT barcode)

components.name: linker
components.role: Read 2 / Index 1 ATACseq primer binding region

components.name: Mosaic End (ME)
components.role: Tn5/TnY transferred mosaic end

oligos.notes: Example barcode (well A1): TCTCGCGT -> GTCTCGTGGGCTCGGCTGTCCCTGTCCTCTCGCGTGCGATCGAGGACGGCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG. 96 barcodes provided in Supplementary Table 2.

oligos.name: MEDS A (double-stranded transposon end, P5 side)
oligos.role: Assembled double-stranded mosaic-end DNA (T5 tagment sciATAC + pMENT) loaded into TnY

components.name: T5 tagment sciATAC (transferred strand)
components.role: 5_to_3 top strand

components.name: pMENT (non-transferred strand)
components.role: ME complement, anneals to the 3' ME

oligos.name: MEDS B (double-stranded barcoded transposon end, P7 side)
oligos.role: Assembled double-stranded mosaic-end DNA (barcoded T7 tagment sciATAC + pMENT) loaded into TnY

components.name: ATAC-seq barcoded tagmentation oligo (transferred strand)
components.role: 5_to_3 top strand, carries BC1

components.name: pMENT (non-transferred strand)
components.role: ME complement, anneals to the 3' ME

oligos.name: RTi sgRNA RT primer
oligos.role: Barcoded in-situ reverse transcription primer for the CROP-seq gRNA transcript; carries the same first-round barcode (BC1) as the matched MEDS B tagmentation oligo. 96 barcoded versions.

components.name: PCR handle
components.role: priming site for P5i sgRNA PCR1 primer

components.name: BC1 RT cell barcode
components.role: first-round combinatorial cell barcode (matched to tagmentation barcode)

components.name: gRNA-scaffold anneal
components.role: primes within the gRNA/tracrRNA scaffold for reverse transcription

oligos.notes: Example barcode TCTCGCGT -> CCGGGAGCTGCATGTTCTCGCGTTTTTTCAAGTTGATAA. Same 8-bp barcode set as the tagmentation oligos.

oligos.name: P5i ATAC-seq PCR1 primer
oligos.role: ATAC-seq library PCR1 P5 primer; adds the P5 adapter and the second-round i5 well barcode onto the T5 handle. Indexed set (~96).

components.name: P5
components.role: Illumina P5

components.name: i5 well barcode (BC2)
components.role: second-round combinatorial cell barcode

components.name: T5 handle anneal
components.role: primes on the T5 tagmentation handle

oligos.name: P7i ATAC-seq PCR1 primer
oligos.role: ATAC-seq library PCR1 P7 primer; adds the P7 adapter and the second-round i7 well barcode onto the T7 handle. Indexed set (~145).

components.name: P7
components.role: Illumina P7

components.name: i7 well barcode (BC3)
components.role: second-round combinatorial cell barcode

components.name: T7 handle anneal
components.role: primes on the T7 tagmentation handle

oligos.name: Read 1 ATACseq
oligos.role: Custom ATAC-seq Read 1 sequencing primer; anneals in the T5 handle/ME and reads the genomic insert from the P5 side

oligos.name: Read 2 ATACseq
oligos.role: Custom ATAC-seq Read 2 sequencing primer; anneals in the T7 handle/ME (3' of BC1) and reads the genomic insert from the P7 side

oligos.name: Index 1 ATACseq
oligos.role: Custom ATAC-seq index sequencing primer; reverse complement of the Read 2 handle, reads the BC1 tagmentation cell barcode

oligos.name: U6 outer sgRNA PCR1
oligos.role: gRNA PCR1 forward outer primer; anneals in the CROP-seq / U6 region upstream of the guide to define the gRNA amplicon

oligos.name: P5i sgRNA PCR1 primer
oligos.role: gRNA PCR1 P5 primer; anneals to the RT-primer handle and adds the second-round i5 well barcode plus a PCR2 handle. Indexed set (~96).

components.name: PCR2 handle
components.role: priming site for P5 sgRNA PCR2 primer

components.name: i5 well barcode (BC2)
components.role: second-round combinatorial cell barcode

components.name: RT-handle anneal
components.role: primes on the RTi sgRNA RT-primer handle

oligos.name: P5 sgRNA PCR2
oligos.role: gRNA PCR2 P5 primer; adds the full P5 adapter and TruSeq Read 1 primer onto the PCR2 handle. Staggered variants (+1/+2/+3/+8 N) add sequence diversity.

components.name: P5
components.role: Illumina P5

components.name: TruSeq Read 1 (partial)
components.role: TruSeq Read 1 primer (with the preceding ACAC forms ACACTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT)

components.name: stagger
components.role: 0/1/2/3/8-nt stagger for base diversity

components.name: PCR2 handle anneal
components.role: primes on the CGCCGTCCAGCTGA handle

oligos.name: P7i sgRNA PCR2 primer
oligos.role: gRNA PCR2 P7 primer; anneals to the gRNA/tracrRNA scaffold and adds the second-round i7 well barcode plus the P7 adapter. Indexed set (~145).

components.name: P7
components.role: Illumina P7

components.name: i7 well barcode (BC3)
components.role: second-round combinatorial cell barcode

components.name: gRNA scaffold anneal
components.role: primes on the sgRNA scaffold (tracrRNA GTGGAAAGGACGAAACAC)

oligos.name: spike-in T5
oligos.role: Spike-in transposon T5 tagmentation oligo (tagmentation control)

oligos.name: spike-in T7
oligos.role: Spike-in transposon T7 tagmentation oligo (tagmentation control)

oligos.name: spike-in P5
oligos.role: Spike-in P5 PCR primer

oligos.name: spike-in P7
oligos.role: Spike-in P7 PCR primer

oligos.name: Illumina P5 adapter
oligos.role: Illumina P5 flow-cell adapter (5' end of the final library)

oligos.name: Illumina P7 adapter
oligos.role: Illumina P7 flow-cell adapter (present as reverse complement at the 3' end of the final library)

oligos.name: Guide RNA protospacer (example, non-targeting 1)
oligos.role: Variable 20-nt CRISPR guide protospacer cloned into CROPseq-Guide-Puro; captured and read out as the perturbation identity
oligos.notes: Represents the variable [FEATURE_BARCODE:20] guide region. 318 guides across 105 genes; example shown is human non-targeting gRNA 1.

annotation_lines: TCGTCGGCAGCGTC = T5 / Nextera P5-side handle (Read 1 ATACseq binding site)
GCCGTCCTCGATCGC = T7-side linker (Read 2 / Index 1 ATACseq binding site)

library_generation.title: Cell fixation, lysis and nuclei isolation
library_generation.summary: Cells are glyoxal-fixed and gently lysed to recover intact nuclei containing genomic DNA and the CROP-seq gRNA transcript.
library_generation.note: Cas9-expressing cells transduced with a lentiviral CROPseq-Guide-Puro gRNA library are fixed for 7 min in glyoxal/ethanol/acetic acid (better nuclear preservation than paraformaldehyde), washed, and lysed with Tween-20/Igepal to yield nuclei; RiboLock preserves the Pol II gRNA transcript for later RT.
library_generation.product:
[ nucleus ]
  genomic DNA (open chromatin)  +  CROP-seq Pol II gRNA transcript

library_generation.title: Barcoded tagmentation (plate 1, well-specific BC1)
library_generation.summary: TnY transposomes loaded with MEDS A (T5) and barcoded MEDS B (T7) fragment open chromatin and add well-specific barcode BC1.
library_generation.note: In each of 96 wells, nuclei are tagmented for 30 min at 37C by TnY (ViPar P50K/M53Q) carrying MEDS A + a unique barcoded MEDS B. Both fragment ends receive a mosaic end; the P7 end also carries the 8-bp first-round cell barcode BC1. EDTA stops the reaction.
library_generation.product:
5'- TCGTCGGCAGCGTCTCCACGCAACCAACGCACCGTCTCCGCCTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG[CDNA]CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTGCCGTCCTCGATCGC[CELL_BARCODE:8]GGACAGGGACAGCCGAGCCCACGAGAC -3'
    |____ T5 handle ____|                       |________ ME ________|   |__ ME __|         |_linker_|     |__ BC1 __|     |____ T7 handle ____|

library_generation.title: In-situ reverse transcription of the gRNA (same well, matched BC1)
library_generation.summary: The gRNA transcript is reverse-transcribed in situ with a barcoded RT primer carrying the same BC1 as the tagmentation oligo in that well.
library_generation.note: RevertAid RT extends the RTi sgRNA RT primer, which anneals within the gRNA/tracrRNA scaffold; the resulting cDNA carries the matched BC1 barcode and a CCGGGAGCTGCATGT handle, linking gRNA identity to the same cell barcode as the ATAC fragments.
library_generation.product:
5'- CCGGGAGCTGCATGT[CELL_BARCODE:8]TTTTTCAAGTTGATAA......[FEATURE_BARCODE:20]......(U6 / CROP-seq) -3'
                                    <------- reverse transcription (gRNA cDNA)
3'- ......................... CROP-seq Pol II gRNA transcript ......................... -5'

library_generation.title: Pool and resplit nuclei (plate 2)
library_generation.summary: All barcoded nuclei are pooled, counted, and redistributed at ~20 nuclei/well into a second plate to define the second combinatorial index.
library_generation.note: Pooling then resplitting means each plate-2 well contains a random mix of BC1 barcodes; the plate-2 well position supplies the second-round i5/i7 indices, so BC1 x i5 x i7 uniquely identifies each cell.
library_generation.product: ~20 nuclei per plate-2 well  (BC1-tagged ATAC fragments + BC1-tagged gRNA cDNA)

library_generation.title: Nuclei digestion / DNA release
library_generation.summary: Nuclei are digested with Proteinase K to release barcoded ATAC fragments and gRNA cDNA for PCR.
library_generation.note: Rapid-extract buffer + SDS/Proteinase K at 65C liberate the templates; PMSF quenches the protease before PCR.
library_generation.product: free BC1-barcoded ATAC fragments  +  free BC1-barcoded gRNA cDNA

library_generation.title: gRNA PCR1 (add i5 / BC2)
library_generation.summary: The gRNA cDNA is amplified with the U6 outer primer and the P5i sgRNA PCR1 primer, adding the second-round i5 barcode and a PCR2 handle.
library_generation.note: PfuX7 amplification (14-18 cycles). P5i sgRNA PCR1 primes on the RT handle and appends i5 (BC2) + CGCCGTCCAGCTGA; the U6 outer primer defines the guide-containing amplicon.
library_generation.product:
5'- CGCCGTCCAGCTGA[CELL_BARCODE:8]CCGGGAGCTGCATGT[CELL_BARCODE:8]TTTTTCAAGTTGATAA...[FEATURE_BARCODE:20]...GTGGAAAGGACGAAACAC... -3'
    |__ PCR2 handle __||__ i5(BC2) __||__ RT handle __||__ BC1 __|                  guide                  scaffold

library_generation.title: gRNA PCR2 (add P5+Read1 and i7+P7) -> final gRNA library
library_generation.summary: A second PCR adds the full P5/TruSeq-Read1 (staggered) on one end and i7+P7 on the scaffold end, completing the sequenceable gRNA library.
library_generation.note: Staggered P5 sgRNA PCR2 primers (0/1/2/3/8-nt) add base diversity; P7i sgRNA PCR2 anneals on the tracrRNA scaffold and adds i7 (BC3) + P7. The final gRNA amplicon (~250 bp) reads out the perturbation and the BC1 x i5 x i7 cell barcode.
library_generation.product:
5'- AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACACTCTTTCCCTACACGACGCTCTTCCGATCT[SPACER:0]CGCCGTCCAGCTGA[CELL_BARCODE:8]CCGGGAGCTGCATGT[CELL_BARCODE:8]TTTTTCAAGTTGATAA...[FEATURE_BARCODE:20]...GTGTTTCGTCCTTTCCACAA[CELL_BARCODE:8]ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG -3'
    |___ P5 ___|                 |___ TruSeq Read 1 ___|                |PCR2 hdl|    |i5(BC2)|       |RT hdl|       |BC1|                              guide                                      |i7(BC3)|       |__ revcomp P7 __|

library_generation.title: ATAC PCR1 (add P5+i5 and P7+i7) -> final ATAC library
library_generation.summary: The tagmented genomic fragments are amplified with P5i and P7i ATAC-seq PCR1 primers, adding P5+i5 and P7+i7 to complete the sequenceable ATAC library.
library_generation.note: PfuX7 amplification. P5i ATAC-seq PCR1 primes the T5 handle and adds P5 + i5 (BC2); P7i ATAC-seq PCR1 primes the T7 handle and adds P7 + i7 (BC3). Combined with BC1 from tagmentation this gives the full BC1 x i5 x i7 cell barcode. This product equals the final ATAC library.
library_generation.product:
5'- AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACAC[CELL_BARCODE:8]TCGTCGGCAGCGTCTCCACGCAACCAACGCACCGTCTCCGCCTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG[CDNA]CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTGCCGTCCTCGATCGC[CELL_BARCODE:8]GGACAGGGACAGCCGAGCCCACGAGAC[CELL_BARCODE:8]ATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG -3'
    |____ P5 ____|               |i5(BC2)|       |_____ T5 handle + ME _____|                                   [CDNA]|__ ME __|         |linker|       |BC1|           |_ T7 handle _|            |i7(BC3)|       |_ revcomp P7 _|

library_sequencing.note: Custom primer matches the T5 handle/mosaic end on the top strand, anneals to the bottom strand and synthesizes rightward into the genomic insert. Genomic read length not stated in the paper text (read lengths given only in Supplementary Fig. 1d,e); ~50 bp shown as typical.
library_sequencing.diagram:
Read 1 primer = top-strand sequence; reads --> into insert
    CACCGTCTCCGCCTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG------->
5'- CACCGTCTCCGCCTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAGNNNN...NNNN -3'  (open chromatin)
3'- GTGGCAGAGGCGGAGTCTACACATATTCTCTGTCNNNN...NNNN -5'

library_sequencing.note: Reverse complement of the T7 mosaic-end/linker; matches the top strand ending just 5' of BC1, anneals to the bottom strand and reads the 8-bp first-round tagmentation barcode.
library_sequencing.diagram:
Index 1 primer = top-strand sequence; reads --> into BC1
    ...ATACACATCTGCCGTCCTCGATCGC------->
5'- ...ATACACATCTGCCGTCCTCGATCGCNNNNNNNNGGACAGGGACAG... -3'
3'- ...TATGTGTAGACGGCAGGAGCTAGCGNNNNNNNNCCTGTCCCTGTC... -5'
                             (BC1, 8 nt)

library_sequencing.note: Standard i5 index read of the 8-bp second-round well barcode added in ATAC PCR1, located between P5 and the T5 handle.
library_sequencing.diagram:
standard i5 index read of the 8-nt i5 (BC2)
5'- AATGATACGGCGACCACCGAGATCTACACNNNNNNNNTCGTCGGCAGCGTC -3'
3'- TTACTATGCCGCTGGTGGCTCTAGATGTGNNNNNNNNAGCAGCCGTCGCAG -5'
                                 (i5, 8 nt)

library_sequencing.note: Standard i7 index read of the 8-bp second-round well barcode added in ATAC PCR1, located between the P7 handle and the P7 adapter.
library_sequencing.diagram:
standard i7 index read of the 8-nt i7 (BC3)
5'- ...CCGAGCCCACGAGACNNNNNNNNATCTCGTATGCCGTCTTCTGCTTG -3'
3'- ...GGCTCGGGTGCTCTGNNNNNNNNTAGAGCATACGGCAGAAGACGAAC -5'
                      (i7, 8 nt)

library_sequencing.note: Custom primer equals the barcoded T7 handle/mosaic end (bottom strand, 3' of BC1); it anneals to the top strand and synthesizes leftward into the genomic insert from the opposite end. gRNA amplicons are sequenced on a separate MiSeq run using the P5/P7 sgRNA primers. Genomic read length not stated in text; ~50 bp shown as typical.
library_sequencing.diagram:
Read 2 primer = bottom-strand sequence; reads <-- into insert
5'- ...(insert)CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTGCCGTCCTCGATCGC... -3'
3'- ...(insert)GACAGAGAATATGTGTAGACGGCAGGAGCTAGCG... -5'
    <-------3'-GACAGAGAATATGTGTAGACGGCAGGAGCTAGCG-5' (Read 2 primer)

title: CRISPR-sciATAC
description: CRISPR-sciATAC is a scalable, single-cell combinatorial-indexing assay for transposase-accessible chromatin (sciATAC) coupled to pooled CRISPR screening. Open chromatin in fixed nuclei is barcoded by in-well tagmentation with a Vibrio parahemolyticus transposase (TnY) loaded with mosaic-end adapters, while the Cas9 guide RNA (expressed from a CROP-seq Pol II transcript) is captured in the same well by barcoded in-situ reverse transcription. Nuclei are pooled, resplit, and both the ATAC fragments and the gRNA amplicons receive a second combinatorial index (i5/i7) in PCR, so a unique BC1×i5×i7 cell barcode jointly tags each cell's chromatin-accessibility fragments and its perturbing gRNA. Genomic reads report chromatin accessibility genome-wide; the gRNA read reports which perturbation each cell received.

original_publication.title: Profiling the genetic determinants of chromatin accessibility with scalable single-cell CRISPR screens

throughput.summary: ~30,000 single cells profiled for joint chromatin accessibility and CRISPR perturbation; 28,510 cells analyzed across two screens using 318 gRNAs targeting 105 genes.
throughput.cells: 28,510 cells analyzed (chromatin-modifier screen: 11,104 cells, median 1,977 unique ATAC fragments/cell; chromatin-remodeling-complex screen: 16,676 cells). ~sevenfold more cells than Perturb-ATAC.
throughput.rna: gRNA capture via in-situ RT of the CROP-seq Pol II transcript; 15,824 cells with >=100 gRNA reads; 83% of barcodes had a single gRNA, 90% with a gRNA at >=99% purity.
throughput.dna: sciATAC (open-chromatin) fragments; comparable yield to published sciATAC datasets; bulk-correlated accessibility profiles.

publication.statistical_model: gRNA enrichment/dropout modeled with MAGeCK (negative binomial) plus robust rank aggregation and Bonferroni correction; differential accessibility assessed with per-cell fraction-in-peak z-scores tested by two-tailed t-test / Wilcoxon rank-sum with Benjamini-Hochberg FDR; chromVAR used for JASPAR motif deviation z-scores.

other.label: Transposase
other.value: TnY - an easy-to-purify Vibrio parahemolyticus (ViPar) transposase (P50K, M53Q) compatible with Tn5 mosaic ends

other.label: Fixation
other.value: Glyoxal fixation (better nuclear preservation than paraformaldehyde)

other.label: Polymerase
other.value: Custom PfuX7 DNA polymerase used for all library PCR

other.label: Design feature
other.value: Staggered P5 sgRNA oligos introduce base diversity for the gRNA amplicon read

other.label: Cost / time
other.value: ~20x lower reagent cost and ~14x less time than Perturb-ATAC

other.label: Data availability
other.value: NCBI GEO GSE161002 (BioProject PRJNA674902); code at gitlab.com/sanjanalab/crispr-sciatac

data_processing.summary: gRNA and ATAC reads are demultiplexed by the combinatorial BC1×i5×i7 cell barcode and aligned separately — gRNA to a custom guide reference, ATAC to the genome. After filtering and deduplication, one gRNA is assigned per cell while ATAC fragments are aggregated into a pseudobulk peak set and per-cell accessibility features, which feed differential-accessibility, motif-deviation, and gRNA-enrichment analyses.
data_processing.statistical_model: Negative binomial (MAGeCK) for gRNA enrichment/essentiality with robust rank aggregation and Bonferroni correction; two-tailed t-test / Wilcoxon rank-sum with Benjamini–Hochberg FDR for differential accessibility; label-permutation test for nucleosome peak shifts.
